Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: The first symptoms are small, olive-green spots on leaves. As they age, these spots become dark brown to black and feel velvety. Severely infected leaves may turn yellow and drop off by mid-summer. On fruit, the spots are brown, corky, and 'scabby' in appearance, and can cause the fruit to crack or become deformed.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment:
1. Remove fallen leaves and infected fruits.
2. Prune branches for airflow.
3. Apply copper, chlorothalonil, or mancozeb fungicides at spring bud-break.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Dark, circular lesions on leaves with purple margins. Fruit shows black, rotten spots that expand and form concentric rings. Branch cankers may develop.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment:
1. Remove and destroy infected leaves and mummified fruits.
2. Prune cankered branches.
3. Apply copper-based fungicides early in season.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright yellow-orange leaf spots. Underside develops tube-like orange spore horns. Fruit may deform.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment:
1. Remove nearby cedar trees if possible.
2. Apply sulfur or copper fungicides at pink-bud stage.
3. Maintain airflow by pruning.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Apple___healthy.symptoms: Leaves are a uniform green color. No visible spots, discoloration, or deformities. Plant appears vigorous.
Apple___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue good watering, fertilization, and pruning practices.
Apple___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Blueberry___healthy.symptoms: Leaves are glossy green and firm, stems strong, berries healthy.
Blueberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain regular mulching and watering.
Blueberry___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White, powdery fungal growth on upper leaf surfaces. Leaves curl and distort. Can affect fruit.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.treatment:
1. Increase air circulation by pruning.
2. Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate sprays.
3. Avoid overhead watering.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.symptoms: Leaves are smooth and green, fruit firm and disease-free.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain proper watering and pruning.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Long, narrow rectangular lesions that are tan to gray in color. Lesions may merge and kill leaf tissue.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment:
1. Rotate crops and avoid corn-on-corn planting.
2. Use resistant hybrids.
3. Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides if early infection.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Brick-red oval pustules on both leaf surfaces; may turn brown to black later.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment:
1. Plant resistant hybrids.
2. Remove alternate hosts.
3. Spray mancozeb or chlorothalonil if severe.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long grayish-green elliptical lesions on leaves, turning tan with dark borders.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment:
1. Grow resistant hybrids.
2. Rotate crops and remove debris.
3. Fungicide: Propiconazole or strobilurin at early signs.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.symptoms: Uniform green leaves with no spots; strong stem growth.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown leaf spots with black edges. Berries develop black, shriveled mummies.
Grape___Black_rot.treatment:
1. Remove infected mummies and leaves.
2. Apply sulfur or mancozeb fungicides.
3. Prune for airflow.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Dark streaks inside wood, berries show black spotting and cracking. Leaves show scorched margins.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment:
1. Remove infected wood.
2. Improve soil drainage.
3. Use fungicides only preventively; severe vines may need removal.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Angular dark-brown leaf spots that merge and cause leaf drop.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment:
1. Remove diseased leaves.
2. Apply copper fungicides.
3. Maintain vineyard airflow.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Grape___healthy.symptoms: No visible spots, mold, or discoloration; normal growth.
Grape___healthy.treatment: Routine pruning, drip irrigation, balanced fertilizer.
Grape___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Blotchy yellow leaf mottling, small bitter fruits, premature fruit drop, twig dieback.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment:
1. Remove infected trees (no cure).
2. Control psyllid insect carrier.
3. Apply micronutrient foliar supplements to slow decline.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small purple-brown leaf spots, fruit pitting and cracking, twig cankers.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.treatment:
1. Use resistant varieties.
2. Apply copper bactericide in early season.
3. Remove infected twigs.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Peach___healthy.symptoms: Smooth green leaves, clean fruit, no lesions.
Peach___healthy.treatment: Maintain orchard hygiene and proper irrigation.
Peach___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Water-soaked leaf spots turning brown; fruit shows raised scabby spots.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment:
1. Remove infected leaves.
2. Apply copper sprays.
3. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.symptoms: Glossy green leaves, firm fruits, no spots.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Brown leaf spots with concentric rings; lower leaves yellow and drop.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment:
1. Remove plant debris.
2. Use chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicides.
3. Rotate crops.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked leaf spots turning brown-purple; white mold underside.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment:
1. Preventive fungicides (mancozeb, zineb).
2. Destroy infected tubers.
3. Harvest after vines die completely.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Potato___healthy.symptoms: Green vigorous leaves; no dark spots or wilting.
Potato___healthy.treatment: Maintain soil drainage and crop rotation.
Potato___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Raspberry___healthy.symptoms: Healthy green leaves, firm canes, normal fruit.
Raspberry___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Soybean___healthy.symptoms: Uniform leaf color and structure; healthy pods.
Soybean___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powder on leaves; leaves yellow and dry early.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.treatment:
1. Spray sulfur or neem oil.
2. Ensure airflow; avoid overhead watering.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Small purple leaf spots turning brown; leaf edges dry and curl.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.treatment:
1. Remove infected leaves.
2. Improve spacing/airflow.
3. Apply copper fungicide if severe.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Strawberry___healthy.symptoms: Green leaves and healthy red berries; no spotting.
Strawberry___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Dark water-soaked leaf spots; fruits get raised brown scabs.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment:
1. Copper sprays weekly.
2. Remove infected leaves.
3. Avoid overhead watering.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings; yellowing lower leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment:
1. Remove lower leaves.
2. Use chlorothalonil or mancozeb sprays.
3. Mulch soil to prevent splash infection.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark greasy spots; white mold beneath leaves.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment:
1. Immediate fungicide: chlorothalonil or copper.
2. Destroy infected plants.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Yellow upper leaf spots; olive-green underside mold.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment:
1. Reduce humidity.
2. Remove lower leaves.
3. Copper fungicide.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Tiny circular spots with dark border and gray center; defoliation common.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment:
1. Remove infected leaves.
2. Spray copper or chlorothalonil.
3. Mulch to prevent soil splash.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Tiny yellow speckling on leaves; fine webbing under leaves; leaves dry and drop.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment:
1. Spray neem oil or insecticidal soap.
2. Increase humidity slightly.
3. Hose off mites underside of leaves.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings, leaf yellowing and dropping.
Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment:
1. Remove infected leaves.
2. Apply copper or mancozeb sprays.
3. Ensure airflow.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Yellow curled leaves, stunted growth, reduced fruit, whiteflies present.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment:
1. Remove infected plants.
2. Control whiteflies (sticky traps, neem).
3. Plant resistant varieties.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mosaic yellow-green pattern on leaves; distorted foliage; reduced fruit.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment:
1. Remove infected plants.
2. Sanitize tools.
3. Use virus-free seeds and resistant varieties.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.

Tomato___healthy.symptoms: Dark green leaves, strong stems, regular fruiting.
Tomato___healthy.prevention: General prevention: Maintain field hygiene, ensure proper spacing for good airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops annually, and use resistant varieties when possible.
